page: Amazon Web Services i3.xlarge is a storage-optimized instance utilizing the Xen hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz, offering 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading. The CPU features a 45 MB L3 cache and supports AVX, AES, and FMA instructions. The instance is configured with 30.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.62 GB per core, and includes a bundled 950 GB local NVMe SSD. Network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the compute capabilities of this instance are modest. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests place the instance in the bottom 10% of benchmarked hardware. Similarly, Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores of 898.0 and 1900.0 fall into the bottom 10% category. Memory bandwidth tests show weak performance, with read-only large block transfers reaching 22.62 GB/sec and write operations at 14.23 GB/sec. Redis SET operations achieve 2.54 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput is measured at 2.81 GB/sec, both ranking in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the hardware profile of the i3.xlarge emphasizes high local storage capacity and memory density over raw processing power. The inclusion of a 950 GB NVMe SSD provides significant local storage resources, though the older Intel Xeon architecture results in lower compute-to-cost efficiency compared to newer generations. This instance is designed for workloads requiring high-speed local storage and moderate memory capacity, such as transactional databases, NoSQL databases, and caching layers, rather than compute-intensive tasks or large language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services i3.xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor at 2.3 GHz, paired with 30.0 GB of RAM. A key hardware feature is the bundled 950 GB local NVMe SSD, which provides high-speed local storage capacity. Compute and memory benchmarks place this instance in the lower tiers, with stress-ng and Geekbench scores falling in the bottom 10%, and memory bandwidth tests ranking in the bottom 25%. Consequently, the instance offers high storage density but lower compute efficiency relative to its resource class. It is best suited for storage-sensitive workloads such as NoSQL databases, transactional databases, and caching systems that benefit from local NVMe storage rather than high-performance CPU processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i3.xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 30.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 950 GB local NVMe SSD for database and storage-focused workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i3.xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 30 GB RAM, and a bundled 950 GB local NVMe SSD.
tagline: A storage-optimized instance featuring dedicated processing, substantial memory capacity, and a high-speed local NVMe SSD for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 45 MB L3 cache
30.0 GB of system memory providing 7.62 GB per physical core
Bundled 950 GB local NVMe SSD for high-speed storage access
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth utilizing the Xen hypervisor
Suited for NoSQL databases, transactional databases, and caching layers
